EE5203 Microprocessors - Week 6 Homework

One External Interrupt, One Flag, One Reliable Action

Author

Basri Erdogan

1 Purpose

This homework reinforces the exact interrupt path used in the laboratory. You will explain the onboard button circuit, repair a short interrupt program, and make one small extension to your working project.

You are not expected to build an animation, classify long presses, or write EXTI registers directly.

2 Part A — Trace the button press

Answer each question in one or two direct sentences.

  1. What voltage level does PC13 read while B1 is released?
  2. What voltage level does PC13 read while B1 is pressed?
  3. Does a press create a rising edge or a falling edge?
  4. Why is GPIO_NOPULL correct for the onboard B1 button?
  5. Which EXTI line belongs to PC13?
  6. Why must EXTI line[15:10] interrupts be enabled in the NVIC?
  7. What runs first after the interrupt request: EXTI15_10_IRQHandler() or HAL_GPIO_EXTI_Callback()?
  8. Why should the callback set a flag instead of doing long work?

3 Part B — Repair the program

The following program intends to toggle LD2 once for every valid B1 press. It contains six mistakes.

uint8_t button_event = 0;
uint32_t last_press_ms = 0;

void HAL_GPIO_EXTI_Callback(uint16_t GPIO_Pin)
{
    if (GPIO_Pin == GPIO_PIN_5) {
        uint32_t now = HAL_GetTick();

        if ((now - last_press_ms) < 80) {
            button_event = 1;
        }
    }
}

while (1)
{
    if (button_event == 0) {
        HAL_GPIO_TogglePin(GPIOC, GPIO_PIN_13);
    }
}

Submit:

  1. A list naming the six mistakes.
  2. A corrected version of the code.
  3. One sentence explaining the purpose of the 80 ms test.

Your correction must:

  • declare the shared event flag correctly;
  • compare the callback argument with the B1 pin;
  • accept an event only after the debounce interval;
  • update the stored timestamp;
  • consume the event once in main;
  • toggle LD2 on PA5.

4 Part C — Extend your laboratory project

Modify the working Week 6 lab project so LD2 toggles only after three valid button presses.

Use these variables:

volatile uint8_t button_event = 0;
uint32_t last_press_ms = 0;
uint8_t press_count = 0;

Requirements:

  • keep PC13 in falling-edge interrupt mode with no internal pull;
  • keep the 80 ms debounce check in the callback;
  • let the callback only update the timestamp and event flag;
  • increment press_count in the main loop;
  • toggle LD2 when press_count == 3, then reset the count to zero;
  • do not use HAL_Delay() in the callback.

5 Evidence

Submit the following:

  1. main.c
  2. the project .ioc file
  3. one screenshot showing the PC13 GPIO and NVIC configuration
  4. a video of at most 30 seconds showing:
    • press 1: no LED change;
    • press 2: no LED change;
    • press 3: LED toggles;
    • the same sequence repeated once.

Name the archive:

EE5203_W06_<StudentNumber>.zip

Add this comment near the top of main.c:

// Student Number: <StudentNumber>
// Video Evidence: <URL>

6 Self-check before submission
